title: ‘Misty Green’ Trailer: Chris Rock Directs and Stars With Rosalind Eleazar, Adam Driver and Anna Kendrick in A24 Drama
description:
A24 has released the trailer for “Misty Green,” a new Hollywood drama written and directed by Chris Rock.

	The film’s logline reads, “Years after h
body:
A24 has released the trailer for “Misty Green,” a new Hollywood drama written and directed by Chris Rock.

	The film’s logline reads, “Years after her Hollywood breakthrough, a talented actress whose career has stalled fights for her second act.” Rosalind Eleazar stars in the titular role, leading a cast of Rock, Adam Driver, Daniel Kaluuya, Anna Kendrick and Topher Grace.

	In the rapid-fire trailer, we see Misty’s agent, played by Grace, tell her, “I get it, it’s tough out there. I don’t even know if I can get you on ‘Law and Order’ right now.” In the next scene, Rock’s character, a filmmaker, tells her, “The last time I cast you, you punched this bitch in the face.” We later see Misty trying to justify her loose lifestyle to a friend, played by Kendrick. She tells her, “I wake up when I wanna wake up. I sleep when I wanna sleep. I get high when I wanna get high, and I fuck when I wanna fuck.” Kendrick’s character then quips back, “I didn’t know you were a Lil Wayne song, that’s so cool.”
	

	“Misty Green” marks Rock’s third feature in the director’s chair. He made his debut in 2003 with “Head of State,” followed by 2007’s “I Think I Love My Wife” and 2014’s “Top Five.” 


	Rock also produced the film alongside David Worthen Brooks and Peter Rice. Miles Alva, Nelson George, Shaum S. Sengupta and Max Henke serve as executive producers.

	Check out the trailer for “Misty Green” below.

schema.headline: ‘Misty Green’ Trailer: Chris Rock Directs and Stars With Rosalind Eleazar, Adam Driver and Anna Kendrick in A24 Drama

title: Buzz Title ‘Third Wheel,’ an ‘Allegory of Post-Colonial Africa,’ Brought to Market at Fantasia’s Frontières (EXCLUSIVE)
description: South Africa’s Totem Zea and Dutch production company PRPL are teaming on “Third Wheel,” one of the big buzz titles this week at Fantasia’s Frontièr
body:
South Africa’s Totem Zea and Dutch production company PRPL are teaming on “Third Wheel,” one of the big buzz titles this week at Fantasia’s Frontières Co-Production Market, combining entertainment in its propulsive psychological thriller drive plus a swingeing comment on post-colonial power dynamics in Africa. 

	To be directed by Zoe Ramushu, whose short “It Takes a Circus” was nominated for the 48th Student Academy Awards, the movie project is produced by her partner Reabetswe Moeti-Vogt at their South African label Totem Zea, behind Black female-fronted comedy “Real Estate Sisters.” 
	

	Also producing is Netherlands-based PRPL, headed by CEO Ellen Havenith, whose “When It Melts” won best performance at Sundance while its “Tiger Stripes” won the Grand Prix at Cannes’ Critics’ Week, both in 2023.


	“Third Wheel” will be presented at the Frontières Co-Production Market, which kicks off in Montreal Wednesday with a day-long pitching session of projects. 

	Also written by Ramushu, “Third Wheel” turns on Thina, 26, a Black surgeon who was adopted by the white family for whom her biological mother still works. 

	At her white adoptive family’s stunning Drakensberg mountain estate, Thina prepares for her wedding with a woke Black fiancé and Emma, her white adoptive sister and best friend. But at a boozy weekend devotion twists into possession and Thina, the synopsis says, “must face the chilling truth: the monster is the one who claims to love you most.”

	“‘Third Wheel’ is an allegory for post-colonial Africa,” Ramushu has said. “Independent countries are dating, getting to know, maybe committing to their governments. The colonizers? Playing third wheel. Told through South Africa’s trend of white families informally adopting Black children — offering ‘what they can’ from the goodness of their white hearts or white guilt. This is one of their stories.”
	

	“We can’t wait to put the soaking wet kudu on the big screen. It quietly stalks the film in a way that’s beautiful, unsettling and uniquely African,” Ramushu told Variety. 

	“Beyond the mystery, ‘Third Wheel’ explores marriage from an unusual angle,” she added. “Weddings force every version of yourself into the same room — your family self, your work self, your childhood self, your partner self. It’s the day you’re supposed to publicly declare who you are and who you belong to… and that can become a nightmare. Literally.”

	Set to shoot in 2027, the project has so far received development and production funding from the Thuthuka Co-Development Fund, the joint support program between South Africa’s National Film and Video Foundation (NFVF) and the Netherlands Film Fund (NFF).

schema.headline: Buzz Title ‘Third Wheel,’ an ‘Allegory of Post-Colonial Africa,’ Brought to Market at Fantasia’s Frontières (EXCLUSIVE)

title: ‘Hell Grind’ AI Studio Higgsfield Debuts Short Film ‘Kök Börü’ at SIGGRAPH (EXCLUSIVE)
description: While Higgsfield AI‘s most recent project, the fully AI-generated absurdist action sci-fi film “Hell Grind,” found some plot in a combination of eth
body:
While Higgsfield AI‘s most recent project, the fully AI-generated absurdist action sci-fi film “Hell Grind,” found some plot in a combination of ethnically vague heroes and monstrous villains, the company’s next project is set in the realm of Central Asian folklore.

	“Kök Börü,” a 15-minute animated short film, marks the studio’s first effort since it premiered “Hell Grind” during May’s Marché du Film, the Cannes Film Festival’s marketplace. The latest project will premiere Wednesday at the SIGGRAPH conference in L.A. “Kök Börü” takes its name from the traditional Central Asian horseback game, where mounted teams try to earn points as they try to drag a goat carcass across a goal line. In the film, nomadic mother Ana tries to find a daughter she lost to a wolf pack years ago after the pack cornered both of her children.
	

	The project, co-directed by Shakhnazar Borboev and Amira Ömirjan, employed a 10-person animation team to bring “Kök Börü” to life within two weeks, a marked difference from the production of “Hell Grind.” The team included three animators, two screenwriters, three sound producers, a sound supervisor and a color artist, while the company hired native Kazakh actors to voice the characters. A combination of Higgsfield tools and Nvidia graphics served as the technical backbone of the production, with animators blending traditional animation techniques with AI.


	It’s a film that Borboev and Ömirjan don’t believe would have been greenlit by a traditional studio due to its Central Asian focus, a barrier they believe AI can help break.
	

	“As artists from Central Asia, this is the kind of story we grew up with but almost never see on screen,” Borboev said in a statement. “Films like this don’t always get the chance to be made, so being able to bring ours to the screen means everything to us.”

	“Stories like ‘Kök Börü’ rarely make it to the screen because they’re often seen as too niche or too risky,” Ömirjan added. “As artists from Central Asia, being able to bring our culture and folklore to audiences means everything to us. AI helped us move faster, while artists remained at the center of every creative decision. This hybrid approach made a film like this possible for a small team like ours.”

	The short film represents the latest effort in AI-focused studios employing hybrid models of production, days after Utopai Studios revealed its upcoming adaptation of “The Most Serious Fart, which will use AI as an assistive tool instead of generating entire visuals. Following the “Kök Börü” premiere at the L.A. conference, the team will lead a panel walking conferencegoers through the production process.

	“Showcasing this film and the pipeline behind it will demonstrate what’s already possible today,” Higgsfield CEO and co-founder Alex Mashrabov said in a statement. “The goal was never to replace artists, but to give more of them the means to tell stories at this level.”

schema.headline: ‘Hell Grind’ AI Studio Higgsfield Debuts Short Film ‘Kök Börü’ at SIGGRAPH (EXCLUSIVE)

title: BAFTA Tweaks Film Awards Rules, Expands Outstanding British Film Longlist and Updates AI Guidance
description: The British Academy has unveiled new changes to its film awards eligibility and voting rules going into the 2027 awards, which are small tweaks comp
body:
The British Academy has unveiled new changes to its film awards eligibility and voting rules going into the 2027 awards, which are small tweaks compared to the substantial edits recently made by AMPAS for the Oscars. 

	Among the major alterations is with the Outstanding British Film category, which now sees the longlist after the first round of voting expanded from 15 films to 20. The category will retain 10 nominations, with five chosen by the membership vote and five by the nominating jury.
	

	“We just wanted to widen the pool for consideration and to get more members watching more British films and voting on them,” said BAFTA‘s executive director of awards and content Emma Baehr. “For us, it’s a celebration — it’s our flagship category.”


	Another tweak comes in the performance categories, where members of BAFTA’s casting chapter will now be invited to vote alongside the acting chapter in the longlisting and nominations rounds. The decision here was more to help “increase the expertise,” says Baehr, who notes that there are more than 700 performance candidates to consider across four categories, which close a week earlier than the others. “So it’s really important that we keep that engagement up.”

	But BAFTA hasn’t gone down the road of the Oscars, which earlier this year made the change to allow performers have multiple nominations in the same category. This was actually permitted at the British Academy previously, most notably when Margot Robbie received two supporting actress nominations in 2020, but was banned following a major review that followed. “That was one our key rule changes — that is not allowed,” said Baehr. “We want to make sure we’re levelling that playing field in those performance categories.”
	

	Given the growing impact of AI, the British Academy has also made further tweaks to clarify its approach to the technology. Having already included the words “human achievement” last year, it has now added this paragraph to it rulebook: “The Awards are presented annually to recognise, honour and reward individuals for outstanding human achievement in originating, shaping and realising the final artistic outcome, regardless of the tools used to bring this work to life. Where the use of Generative AI or other digital tools is relevant to the consideration of the work, BAFTA reserves the right to request additional information regarding the nature of such use and the extent of human creative involvement.”

	According to Baehr, the addition gives BAFTA the “opportunity to just dig in a bit deeper if there’s something we want to check.” But it would be then up to the film committee to decide if a film doesn’t fit in with the eligibility and the rules. Asked whether a fully AI-generated movie or AI performance could ever make it through to the nominations, Baehr said that it “would always be a human that picks up a BAFTA.” (Sorry, Tilly Norwood). 

	Elsewhere, BAFTA is continuing to make bullying and harassment measures policies mandatory for its British categories, a rule which requires production entrants to declare that they’re aligned with standards set by CIISA (Creative Industries Independent Standards Authority). But it’s now requesting information from all submitted productions, although its asserts it’s not mandatory for those outside the British categories. This is something it says will be monitored with the “expectation that this will become a requirement in future years.”

	Alongside the rule changes, the British Academy has also revealed the key dates for the upcoming film awards. Entries open on July 22, with round one voting opening Dec 4. The longlists are published Jan 7, the same day that round two voting opens, and final nominations open Jan 19. . The final round of voting opens Jan 26 with the BAFTA film awards ceremony being held Feb. 21.

schema.headline: BAFTA Tweaks Film Awards Rules, Expands Outstanding British Film Longlist and Updates AI Guidance

title: Audible ‘Dracula’ Adaptation Stars Jonathan Bailey, Ella Purnell, Aimee Lou Wood
description: Bram Stoker’s classic spine-tingler “Dracula” is getting all-new adaptations from Audible — in seven different languages, each with its own full-cas
body:
Bram Stoker’s classic spine-tingler “Dracula” is getting all-new adaptations from Audible — in seven different languages, each with its own full-cast audio recording.

	Audible is adapting the Gothic horror novel for English, Castilian Spanish, French, German, Italian, Latin American Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese. Each production features a full ensemble cast.

	The English-language production of Audible’s “Dracula” stars Jonathan Bailey (“Wicked,” “Bridgerton”) as Dracula, Ella Purnell (“Fallout”) as Mina, Aimee Lou Wood (“The White Lotus”) as Lucy, Louis Partridge (“Enola Holmes”) as Jonathan, David Jonsson (“Industry”) as Dr. Seward, Lesley Manville (“Phantom Thread”) as Mrs. Westenra and Maggie Gyllenhaal (“The Dark Knight”) as Professor Van Helsing.
	

	Leading the international language casts as Dracula and Mina respectively are Omar Sy and Deva Cassel (French); Riccardo Scamarcio and Benedetta Porcaroli (Italian); Matthias Schweighöfer and Jeanne Goursaud (German); Asier Etxeandía and Claudia Salas (Castilian Spanish); and José María de Tavira and Esmeralda Pimentel (LatAm Spanish). 


	Here’s the story of “Dracula,” as summarized by Audible: When her fiancé vanishes in Transylvania, Mina Murray pieces together fragments of letters, diary entries and chilling testimony to hunt the predator stalking those she loves, only to discover he’s already hunting her. When Mina’s closest friend Lucy succumbs to his thrall, the enigmatic Professor Van Helsing is summoned to wage war on this unfathomable threat. But it is Mina, with her intellect, her resolve, her gift for drawing unseen lines between clues, who may hold the key to stopping this ancient predator — if he hasn’t already found his way inside her mind.
	

	Audible’s “Dracula” launches Oct. 1, 2026, for English, French, Italian, Castilian Spanish and LatAm Spanish; the German version will premiere Feb. 9, 2027. Those are available now to preorder on Audible. The Brazilian Portuguese version is slated to debut sometime in 2027; the cast has yet to be announced. 

	Audible’s “Dracula” is directed by Mary Lambert — known for directing film adaptation of Stephen King’s cult horror classic “Pet Sematary” — in her audiobook directorial debut. The novel is adapted by writer Robyn Ahern, recipient of the Bisha K. Ali Screenwriters Fellowship, for her first lead credit. The audiobook features an original score by Ilan Eshkeri (Audible’s “1984,” “House of Guinness”).

	“This is what Audible does best: crafting immersive audio experiences that transport listeners into the heart of the story,” said Aurelie de Troyer, international head of Audible content. “When Dracula whispers in your ear, when you hear his footsteps in the dark but see no reflection, that’s when you understand why we’re the home for this story. Our production elevates the psychological thriller at Dracula’s core, with the battle between Mina and Dracula as the heart of it all.”

	Other Audible Original adaptations include Jane Austen’s “Pride and Prejudice” and George Orwell’s “1984.” In addition, Audible recently released full-cast audiobooks of all seven novels in J.K. Rowling’s “Harry Potter” series.

schema.headline: Audible ‘Dracula’ Adaptation Stars Jonathan Bailey, Ella Purnell, Aimee Lou Wood

title: Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Japanese cult action star Tak Sakaguchi has signed on to play the nemesis of Iko Uwais in “Pendekar: Warrior,” an upcoming martial-arts feature mark
body:
Japanese cult action star Tak Sakaguchi has signed on to play the nemesis of Iko Uwais in “Pendekar: Warrior,” an upcoming martial-arts feature marking Sakaguchi’s first major screen role outside his home country. 

	Principal photography is scheduled to begin in the fourth quarter of 2026.

	Sakaguchi, known to action devotees for his cult roles in “Versus” and “Re:Born,” has built his reputation on an unrelenting physical approach to screen fighting. He is widely associated with one of the more extraordinary sequences in action cinema: a 77-minute single-take fight in “Crazy Samurai Musashi” in which he faces down some 400 opponents. His pairing with Uwais – internationally recognized for “The Raid,” “The Raid 2,” “Headshot,” “The Night Comes for Us,” “Mile 22” and “Expend4bles” – brings together two performers from rival martial-arts traditions who have not previously shared a screen.
	

	“Iko and Tak share something incredibly rare: when they fight on screen, the audience feels that every movement has purpose and consequence,” director, filmmaker and cinematographer John Radel ACS said. “They come from different martial-arts traditions, but they have the same uncompromising commitment to truth in performance. Watching these two finally face one another will be visceral, emotional and unlike anything audiences have seen from either of them before.”


	Set on the streets of Jakarta, “Pendekar: Warrior” centers on a former silat master who leaves prison intent on putting violence behind him, only to find a crime syndicate has taken hold of his old neighborhood. Forced to fight again to protect those closest to him, the character becomes the vehicle through which the film aims to bring Indonesian silat to a global audience. Both Uwais and Sakaguchi are in active physical preparation, working with the production’s action team to develop sequences that blend silat’s speed with Sakaguchi’s close-quarters style.
	

	“This is more than a clash between two great action performers,” Radel added. “We are creating action that comes from character and emotion. When Iko and Tak finally collide, it has to feel like two unstoppable forces meeting, and like a moment action fans have been waiting years to witness.”

	The film is produced by Asia Media Alliance Group alongside leading Indonesian producer Delon Tio of Nation Pictures, Erika North and Nick North of Yeti Entertainment, and veteran action producer Mike Leeder of Hong Kong-based Red Packet Media. Uwais and Ryan Santoso are attached as executive producers through Uwais Pictures, with K-Movie Entertainment serving as co-producer and international sales agent.

	“Bringing Iko Uwais and Tak Sakaguchi together is a landmark moment for Asian action cinema,” Tio said. “They are two extraordinary performers who have each built a global following through authenticity, discipline and fearless physical storytelling. ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ represents exactly the kind of bold, internationally focused Indonesian film we want to bring to audiences around the world.”

	“I’ve been a huge fan of Tak and his work since the early days, going right back to ‘Versus,'” Leeder said. “I’ve always wanted the chance to work with him, and now we get to pit his close-quarters combat against Iko’s silat in one hell of a movie.”

	Uwais is represented by Brett Norensberg at Gersh and Vault Entertainment.

schema.headline: Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE)

title: Emily Watson Joined on Sarajevo Film Festival Jury by Athina Rachel Tsangari, Igor Bezinović, and More
description: The Sarajevo Film Festival has unveiled the jury for its main feature competition, which is headed by actress Emily Watson, as previously announced.
body:
The Sarajevo Film Festival has unveiled the jury for its main feature competition, which is headed by actress Emily Watson, as previously announced.

	Joining Watson are filmmakers Athina Rachel Tsangari and Igor Bezinović, actress Sara Klimoska and CEO of the European Film Academy Matthijs Wouter Knol. The winners will be announced on Aug. 21.

	Tsangari, one of the leaders of the Greek New Wave, broke through with “Attenberg” (2010), which won the Coppa Volpi award for best actor and the Lina Mangiacapre directing award at Venice. “Chevalier” (2015) won best film at the London Film Festival and was nominated for best foreign film at the Independent Spirit Awards. “Harvest,” a neo-western starring Caleb Landry Jones and Harry Melling, premiered in Venice competition in 2024.
	

	Bezinović, from Croatia, is best known for “Fiume o morte!” (2025), which won the European Film Award for best documentary. His other films include the documentaries “The Blockade” (2012) and “Veruda – A Film About Bojan” (2015).


	Klimoska, who is from North Macedonia, has the leading role in “Sister Brother Manhole Cover,” directed by Milcho Manchevski, which is set to premiere this year. Other film credits include Goran Stolevski’s “You Won’t Be Alone,” which premiered at Sundance, and Stolevski’s “Housekeeping for Beginners,” which screened at Venice.

	Before joining the European Film Academy, Wouter Knol was head of programming of Berlinale Talents from 2008 to 2014, and director of the European Film Market from 2014 to 2020.

	The 32nd Sarajevo Film Festival will take place from Aug. 14 to 21.

schema.headline: Emily Watson Joined on Sarajevo Film Festival Jury by Athina Rachel Tsangari, Igor Bezinović, and More

title: Vanessa Marano to Produce and Star in Frontières Horror Comedy ‘Ring Leader’: ‘It’s ‘Bridesmaids’ Meets ‘Self Help’’ (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Vanessa Marano, a prolific actor known for leading roles and guest turns in numerous notable TV series throughout the 2000s, will both produce and s
body:
Vanessa Marano, a prolific actor known for leading roles and guest turns in numerous notable TV series throughout the 2000s, will both produce and star in the bridal-party horror comedy “Ring Leader.”

	Marano is producing the feature through her U.S. production company Calabrian Rhode, behind 2022 Netflix Original romantic comedy “The Royal Treatment,” which hit the streamer’s No. 1 spot worldwide upon its release.


	British Columbia-based director Jason Lapeyre is co-producing via his label Osaka Sunset Pictures. Both are attending the Fantasia Film Festival’s Frontières Co-Production Market where they are presenting the project to genre buyers and industry at its marquee pitch event July 22.
	

	“Our goal is to come out of the market with a partner who can help us complete our gap financing goals,” Marano told Variety. “We will be filming in British Columbia, and the tax incentives coming out of Canada allow for us to make a commercially viable film at a realistic scale without sacrificing passion or vision.”

	Marano, best known for her leading role in five seasons of global TV hit “Switched At Birth” (2011–2017), has been building her career on the other side of the camera over the past several years. She founded Calabrian Rhode in 2018 with her mother and younger sister – respectively, longtime Primetime Emmy consultant for HBO Ellen Marano and singer, producer, and actor Laura Marano, who starred in “The Royal Treatment.”

	Lapeyre’s genre features include “Cold Blooded,” Fantasia’s best Canadian film audience award winner in 2012, and “I Declare War” (2013), which picked up several awards on its festival circuit run.
	

	Marano said she and Lapeyre, who became friends on set in 2013, had been talking about collaborating for years. More recently, she was talking with Elana Dunkelman and her writing partner, Becky Shrimpton about potential projects. “We all got together on a video call and started brainstorming an idea for one of the darkest and most terrifying horror scenarios of all time – a bridal party.

	“Elana and Becky wrote an incredible script that truly captures all the complicated feelings that go into being a bridesmaid—the absolute wild fun and the bone-chilling psychological torment. It’s ‘Bridesmaids’ meets ‘Send Help.’”

	Frontières’s Co-Production Market unspools July 22-25 at the Fantasia Film Festival in Montreal.

schema.headline: Vanessa Marano to Produce and Star in Frontières Horror Comedy ‘Ring Leader’: ‘It’s ‘Bridesmaids’ Meets ‘Self Help’’ (EXCLUSIVE)

title: ‘Seinfeld’ Star Jason Alexander Apologizes to Courtney Stodden for Filming ‘Inappropriate’ Sketch When She Was a Minor: ‘I Truly Regret It’
description: “Seinfeld” alum Jason Alexander issued an apology to reality star Courtney Stodden after she called him out on Instagram for filming an inappropriat
body:
“Seinfeld” alum Jason Alexander issued an apology to reality star Courtney Stodden after she called him out on Instagram for filming an inappropriate comedy sketch her when she was a minor. Stodden was 17 years old when she filmed a Funny or Die sketch with Alexander that parodied “Dr. Phil” and other daytime talk shows. The sketch went online in 2012 and featured Alexander rubbing a cellphone on Stodden’s breasts because it was the only area with “plenty of signal.”
	

	“Looking back at the comedy sketch in which Ms. Stodden and I participated in 2012, I completely agree that it was inappropriate and I truly regret it,” Alexander said in a statement to TMZ. “But more importantly, I am deeply sorry for any harm or distress it has caused Ms. Stodden. I offer her my sincerest apologies.”

	Variety has reached out to Alexander’s representatives for additional statement.


	Stodden resurfaced the sketch in an Instagram post in which she wrote: “I was 17 years old in this photo. The older I get, the more impossible this is to understand. During this comedy sketch, Jason Alexander repeatedly rubbed his phone across my breasts while saying there was ‘no signal’ in my head but ‘plenty of signal’ in my chest. Later in the sketch, he joked that when I turned 18 he’d like to take me behind the couch and ‘have his way’ with me. IMDb credits him as one of the writers of the episode. As an adult, that fact has become harder—not easier—for me to understand. I was a minor. I didn’t have the legal power to decide whether I wanted to participate. The contracts were signed by adults. The paycheck went to [ex-husband Doug Hutchison]. The production moved forward. I was the only child in the room. I felt so alone.”
	

	“Looking back now, I don’t see comedy. I see a 17-year-old girl whose body became part of an adult joke,” Stodden continued. “People still say, ‘You chose to be there.’ Legally, I couldn’t make those decisions for myself. That’s the point. I can’t change what happened to me but I can ask myself: How did so many adults look at a minor and decide this was acceptable? That’s exactly why I speak out today. Children deserve laws that protect them. They shouldn’t have to become adults before realizing the law should have protected them all along.”

	Stodden is currently advocating for California to pass AB1267, a bill that would ban child marriage by making the legal minimum age 18 years old. Stodden wed 51-year-old actor Doug Hutchison in 2011 when she was just 16 years old.

schema.headline: ‘Seinfeld’ Star Jason Alexander Apologizes to Courtney Stodden for Filming ‘Inappropriate’ Sketch When She Was a Minor: ‘I Truly Regret It’

title: ‘Harry Potter’ Back to Hogwarts 2026 Celebrations Feature ‘Sorcerer’s Stone’ 25th Anniversary Rerelease, New Glimpses of HBO Series
description: Warner Bros. Discovery has unveiled its 2026 “Back to Hogwarts” program, a global celebration marking the 25th anniversary of “Harry Potter and the
body:
Warner Bros. Discovery has unveiled its 2026 “Back to Hogwarts” program, a global celebration marking the 25th anniversary of “Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone” with a weeklong theatrical re-release, live fan events across four global regions and new glimpses of the upcoming HBO Original series.

	The celebration centers on Sept. 1, the date on which students traditionally return to Hogwarts in the “Harry Potter” stories, a tradition WBD has turned into a yearly global event uniting fans in cinemas, at live gatherings, online, in stores and at home.
	

	As part of the anniversary, “Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone” – released internationally as “Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone” – returns to theaters for one week with 12 minutes of previously unseen on the big screen behind-the-scenes footage. Separately, all eight “Harry Potter” films will screen in theaters worldwide from Aug. 27 through Sept. 3.


	In London, HBO Max is staging its first-ever Back to Hogwarts celebration on Sept. 1, timed to build anticipation for the HBO Original series’ Christmas debut. The one-day fan event will include interactive moments and giveaways inspired by the new series, with location and ticketing details to be announced. WBD says fans should not expect any event, countdown or announcement that day at King’s Cross Station, and is urging fans to skip a trip there.

	Additional live celebrations are planned in France, Italy, Spain, Germany, China, Australia and Japan. Highlights include a large-scale wand lesson staged in the Orangerie Gardens at Paris’ Château de Versailles, choreographed by Paul Harris, a celebratory screening in Venice, fan festivities in Madrid and Sydney, an event in Berlin, and anniversary activations in China featuring the “Owl Messenger” installation.
	

	Theme park activity runs Aug. 1 through Sept. 1 at Universal Orlando Resort and Universal Studios Hollywood, including anniversary experiences and the return of the “Hogwarts Always” projection show at Orlando’s Wizarding World of Harry Potter – Hogsmeade. Universal Studios Japan will offer select guests after-hours access to its Wizarding World area on Sept. 1, while Universal Beijing Resort will debut its own “Hogwarts Always” projection show alongside a Gathering Night event on Aug. 28.

	Warner Bros. Studio Tour London – The Making of Harry Potter will mark the day with an 11 a.m. announcement broadcast repeated every 30 minutes at Platform 9¾. The tour is running a competition for a personalized Hogwarts trunk autographed by cast members Bonnie Wright, Mark Williams, James and Oliver Phelps, plus two tickets to visit on Sept. 1. Warner Bros. Studio Tour Tokyo – The Making of Harry Potter will hold its own countdown event and photo opportunity. In North America, Harry Potter Shop New York and Harry Potter Shop Chicago will host 11 a.m. countdowns and sell a new Back to Hogwarts collection.

	Mobile games are joining in as well, with “Harry Potter: Hogwarts Mystery” adding new adventures and rewards, and “Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells” running its own countdown events.

	The program culminates in the annual Back to Hogwarts Showcase, streaming live on Sept. 1 via the official Harry Potter YouTube and TikTok channels with announcements from across the franchise.

schema.headline: ‘Harry Potter’ Back to Hogwarts 2026 Celebrations Feature ‘Sorcerer’s Stone’ 25th Anniversary Rerelease, New Glimpses of HBO Series

title: Zohran Mamdani Wants Federal Government To Arrest Benjamin Netanyahu
description:
Zohran Mamdani To The Feds
                          Arrest That 'War Criminal' Bibi Netanyahu!!!
body:
Zohran Mamdani To The Feds
                          Arrest That 'War Criminal' Bibi Netanyahu!!!
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            6:18 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            Zohran Mamdani clearly hates Benjamin "Bibi" Netanyahu ... because he's demanding the federal government arrest the Israeli prime minister if Bibi so much as steps foot in the Big Apple!!!
      
    
            The New York City mayor has been on a tear lately when it comes to Bibi ... telling the New York Times he was exploring ways to arrest Bibi on the local side. But that idea appears to have gone south after talking with his law department and learning he had no legal authority to arrest a foreign government leader.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            You may recall ... In 2024, the International Criminal Court (ICC) issued an arrest warrant for Netanyahu, charging him with war crimes and crimes against humanity in connection with the never-ending conflict in the Gaza Strip.
      

            On Tuesday, Mamdani upped the ante in a video statement, labeling Bibi a "war criminal" and "the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people."
      
    
            The Israelis have been at war with the Palestinians for decades in Gaza.
      
    
            Mamdani also insisted the federal government execute the ICC's warrant and arrest Netanyahu, who the mayor says "is not welcome in New York City, nor is any other war criminal at large."
      
    
            As you know, Bibi is supposed to participate in the 79th Session of the General Assembly of the United Nations in September.
      
    
            If he does show up for the session in Manhattan, one thing's for sure ... he ain't going out for a drink with Mamdani.

title: Kim Delaney's Husband Arrested for Allegedly Being Drunk After Flight Incident
description:
Kim Delaney's Husband
                          Busted for Being Drunk in Public After Flight Dra
body:
Kim Delaney's Husband
                          Busted for Being Drunk in Public After Flight Drama
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            1:03 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 22, 2026
                                5:14 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Kim Delaney's husband, James Morgan, was arrested for public intoxication after allegedly getting into it with the former "NYPD Blue" star and a flight attendant during a recent trip ... TMZ has learned.
      
    
            Law enforcement sources tell TMZ ... Morgan was arrested and booked Monday after landing at Los Angeles International Airport. We're told he and Delaney flew into L.A. from Detroit on a Delta flight, and the couple allegedly got into an argument during the flight.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            We're told Morgan was also involved in a separate incident with a flight attendant. It's unclear what sparked either confrontation.
      

            A Delta spokesperson tells TMZ ... "Delta has zero tolerance for unruly or unlawful behavior, as the safety of our customers and crew always comes first."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            The airline says there was no operational impact to the flight and confirmed law enforcement responded after the plane arrived in Los Angeles.
      
    
            As TMZ previously reported, Morgan was arrested back in February after a disturbance at the couple's Los Angeles-area apartment.

title: 'Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior' Star Mike Preston Dead at 93
description:
'Mad Max 2' Star Mike Preston
                          Reportedly Dead At 93
body:
'Mad Max 2' Star Mike Preston
                          Reportedly Dead At 93
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 22, 2026
                            5:36 AM PDT
                                                                            
              
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Actor Mike Preston -- best known for his iconic role as Pappagallo in "Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior" -- has died.
      
    
            Preston passed away on June 8 in Florida, according to Variety, citing his wife, Josie. His cause of death was not disclosed.
      
    
            Born in London, England in 1938, Preston launched his TV and movie career with the 1969 flick, “Stoney.”
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Years later, Preston scored his biggest role ever in the 1981 classic film, "The Road Warrior," starring Mel Gibson. Preston played Pappagallo, the charismatic leader of a settler group trying to bring peace to the violent wasteland. 
      

            Then, in 1983, Preston secured the role of Tim Thomerson in the sci-fi movie “Metalstorm: The Destruction of Jared-Syn.”
      
    
            The actor also had success on the small screen, scoring the part of Alec Shaw in 8 episodes of the 1984 TV series "Hot Pursuit."
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            During the '80s and '90s, Preston also made cameos in other popular TV shows, such as “Ellen,” “Scarecrow and Mrs. King,” “Shadow Chasers,” “Outlaws,” “Superbly,” “Alien Nation,” “Dark Justice,” “Highlander,” “Steel” and “Law & Order.”
      
    
            And get this ... you can also hear Preston's voice in the 2002 video game, “The Getaway."
      
    
            Preston was 93 and is survived by his wife Josie and their two daughters.
      
    
            RIP

schema.headline: 'Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior' Star Mike Preston Dead at 93
